displayTitle: Who Does What: Levels I, II & III

beats.narration: Ultrasonic testing has a people side: who is allowed to do what. Certification levels draw those lines, from trainee to Level Three.

payload.title: WHO DOES WHAT
payload.sub: Certification levels draw the lines of authority.

beats.narration: Three certified levels share the work. Level One tests to written instructions. Level Two interprets and evaluates results. Level Three approves the procedures.
Duties stack as you climb. A trainee works only under direct supervision. Level One stays inside the instruction — interpretation begins at Level Two.
SNT TC One A recommends. Your employer's written practice makes it binding, and your employer issues the certification. Whichever document the contract invokes governs.
So here's the rule: know your lane, work it well, respect the boundary. It exists so every judgment comes from someone qualified.

payload.sub: Every judgment made by someone qualified to make it.
